writeContent.line6: Include a compelling introduction that hooks the reader
writeContent.line7: Use clear headings and subheadings for structure
writeContent.line8: Include relevant examples, data, or anecdotes
writeContent.line9: End with a strong conclusion and call to action
writeContent.line10: Optimize for readability (short paragraphs, varied sentence length)

writeEmail.line4: Include a clear, descriptive subject line
writeEmail.line5: Open with an appropriate greeting for the relationship
writeEmail.line6: Get to the point in the first paragraph — state the purpose clearly
writeEmail.line7: If requesting action, make the ask specific and include a deadline
writeEmail.line8: Keep it concise — aim for 5-8 sentences maximum for the body
writeEmail.line9: End with a clear next step or call to action
writeEmail.line10: Include an appropriate sign-off matching the tone

writeBlog.line4: Write an SEO-friendly title that's compelling and under 60 characters
writeBlog.line5: Start with a hook — a surprising fact, question, or bold statement
writeBlog.line6: Use H2/H3 subheadings every 200-300 words to improve scannability
writeBlog.line7: Include specific examples, data points, or case studies to support claims
writeBlog.line8: Add a practical takeaway or actionable tip in each major section
writeBlog.line9: End with a compelling conclusion and a clear call to action
writeBlog.line10: Optimize for readability: short paragraphs (2-3 sentences), varied sentence length, transition words

writeDocumentation.line4: Start with a clear overview: what it does, who it's for, and prerequisites
writeDocumentation.line5: Include a quick start section that gets users running in under 5 minutes
writeDocumentation.line6: Provide working code examples for every feature — copy-pasteable, not pseudocode
writeDocumentation.line7: Document parameters, return values, and error cases for each endpoint/function
writeDocumentation.line8: Add a troubleshooting section with common errors and their solutions
writeDocumentation.line9: Include a changelog or version compatibility notes
writeDocumentation.line10: Use consistent formatting: code blocks for code, tables for parameters, admonitions for warnings

writeProposal.line3: Start with a compelling executive summary (one page max)
writeProposal.line4: Clearly define the problem or opportunity being addressed
writeProposal.line5: Present the proposed solution with specific deliverables and milestones
writeProposal.line6: Include a realistic timeline with phases and key dates
writeProposal.line7: Provide a detailed budget breakdown with justification for each line item
writeProposal.line8: Quantify the expected ROI or benefits with concrete metrics
writeProposal.line9: Address risks and mitigation strategies proactively
writeProposal.line10: End with clear next steps and a specific call to action

writeStory.line4: Create a compelling opening that immediately draws the reader in
writeStory.line5: Develop distinct characters with clear motivations and flaws
writeStory.line6: Build the world through sensory details — show, don't tell
writeStory.line7: Include rising tension with at least one major conflict or turning point
writeStory.line8: Write natural dialogue that reveals character and advances the plot
writeStory.line9: Create a satisfying ending that resolves the central conflict
writeStory.line10: Vary sentence rhythm — mix short punchy sentences with longer descriptive ones

writeSocialMedia.line4: Hook the reader in the first line — make them stop scrolling
writeSocialMedia.line5: Keep it platform-appropriate: respect character limits and format norms
writeSocialMedia.line6: Use line breaks and whitespace for readability on mobile
writeSocialMedia.line7: Include a clear call-to-action (like, share, comment, visit link)
writeSocialMedia.line8: Suggest 3-5 relevant hashtags at the end
writeSocialMedia.line9: Write 3 variations with different angles for A/B testing
writeSocialMedia.line10: Optimize for engagement: ask a question or share a contrarian take

writeResume.line4: Start with a compelling professional summary (3-4 lines max)
writeResume.line5: Use strong action verbs: led, built, increased, reduced, launched
writeResume.line6: Quantify achievements with specific numbers, percentages, or dollar amounts
writeResume.line7: Tailor keywords to match the job description for ATS optimization
writeResume.line8: Keep it to one page (two pages max for 10+ years experience)
writeResume.line9: List skills as a concise keyword section — not long descriptions
writeResume.line10: Prioritize recent and relevant experience — cut anything older than 10 years

writeReport.line4: Start with an executive summary — key findings and recommendations in one paragraph
writeReport.line5: Use clear section headers: Background, Methodology, Findings, Recommendations
writeReport.line6: Present data with tables and bullet points — avoid long prose for numbers
writeReport.line7: Include specific metrics, KPIs, and comparisons to benchmarks or past periods
writeReport.line8: Highlight risks and issues with clear severity levels and owners
writeReport.line9: End with actionable recommendations prioritized by impact
writeReport.line10: Add an appendix for detailed data, charts, or supporting evidence

writeCopy.line4: Lead with the biggest benefit — what problem does it solve for the reader?
writeCopy.line5: Use the AIDA framework: Attention, Interest, Desire, Action
writeCopy.line6: Write a headline that creates urgency or curiosity
writeCopy.line7: Focus on benefits, not features — translate specs into outcomes
writeCopy.line8: Include social proof: numbers, testimonials, or authority signals
writeCopy.line9: End with a single, clear call-to-action — don't give multiple choices
writeCopy.line10: Write 3 variations: short (tagline), medium (social ad), and long (landing page)

writeSpeech.line4: Open with a powerful hook: a surprising fact, a personal story, or a provocative question
writeSpeech.line5: Structure around 3 main points — audiences remember threes
writeSpeech.line6: Write for the ear, not the eye — use short sentences and conversational language
writeSpeech.line7: Include pauses, transitions, and emphasis markers for delivery
writeSpeech.line8: Add a memorable story or analogy for each key point
writeSpeech.line9: Build to a climax — save the most impactful message for the end
writeSpeech.line10: Close with a clear call to action or a memorable one-liner the audience will repeat
